# Notch So Good

> A pixel-art crab called Chawd lives in your MacBook's notch and watches Claude Code so you don't have to.

## What it is

Notch So Good is a free, open-source macOS menubar app for Claude Code users. It places a tiny animated pixel-art crab mascot (Chawd) in your MacBook's notch area. When Claude Code is running, a black pill extends the notch showing Chawd and a live session timer. When Claude Code needs your input, the notch expands into a color-coded notification. Click to jump back to your terminal.

## Key features

- Session pill: black pill extends the notch with Chawd mascot + live timer
- 13 idle animations: wave, dance, sneeze, peek-a-boo, yawn, hiccup, spin, stretch, levitate, shiver, strut, nod, walk
- Mouse-reactive eyes: Chawd's pixel eyes track your cursor
- Drowsiness system: Chawd falls asleep after 2 minutes idle, startled wake-up on hover
- Color-coded notifications: green (complete), blue (question), amber (permission), purple (general)
- Multi-session: hover pill to see all active Claude Code sessions grouped by project
- Auto-update via Sparkle

## Install

npx notch-so-good

Or: curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/deepshal99/notch-so-good/main/get.sh | bash

## Requirements

- macOS 14+ (Sonoma or later)
- Claude Code
- MacBook with a notch (recommended)

## How it works

Uses Claude Code hooks (SessionStart, Notification, Stop) via URL scheme callbacks. No server, no polling, no network requests. The hooks send events to notchsogood:// URLs which the app handles natively.

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/deepshal99/notch-so-good
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/notch-so-good
- License: MIT
